# CI Validation Checklist: Maintainer and Contributor Release Workflow

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-009 requirements distinguish current PR Checks behavior, local docs-site validation, and release-readiness expectations without promising future DOC-010 CI behavior.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` phase based on feature context and requirements.

## Current PR Checks Behavior

- [x] CHK001 Does the spec require docs-only PR Checks behavior to be sourced from `.github/workflows/pr-checks.yml`? [Spec §FR-009, Spec §AC-9.6]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec distinguish skipped plugin matrix behavior from still-relevant `validate-pr-title` and `validate-plugins` sentinel behavior? [Spec §User Story 4, Spec §Clarifications Session 2]
- [x] CHK003 Does the plan avoid claiming that current CI runs the full shell suite for every PR? [Spec §Clarifications Session 3, Research §Decision 5]

## Docs-Site Validation

- [x] CHK004 Does the spec require `pnpm --dir docs-site validate` for any `docs-site/**` change? [Spec §FR-008, Spec §Clarifications Session 3]
- [x] CHK005 Does the spec require validation wording to cite `docs-site/package.json` for `reference:check`, `check`, and `build`? [Spec §FR-008, Research §Decision 4]
- [x] CHK006 Does the quickstart include both `reference:check` and `validate` so generated reference drift and full docs-site validation are independently reviewable? [Quickstart §Validation Commands]

## Release Readiness

- [x] CHK007 Does the spec present `bash tests/speckit-pro/run-all.sh` as the release-readiness expectation? [Spec §FR-007, Spec §AC-9.2]
- [x] CHK008 Do mixed docs-site plus plugin or release-surface PRs require both validation lanes and generated/synchronized evidence where relevant? [Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Clarifications Session 3]
- [x] CHK009 Does DOC-009 hand future docs-site CI enforcement to DOC-010 instead of implementing or promising it? [Spec §FR-014, Plan §Summary]

## Findings

- No gap markers found.
- No consensus escalation required.
